channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Samsung has a 16TB PCIe 6.0 SSD coming soon with read/write speeds of 28.4GBps and 21.9GBps respectively — but you won't be able to use it anyway
item.description: Samsung's new 16TB SSD reads at 28.4 GB/s, but you can't plug it into a desktop PC just yet.

item.title: Nothing’s good-looking Watch 3 Pro smartwatch is just $69
item.description: While most fitness trackers are losing the screens to keep the price low, the CMF by Nothing Watch 3 Pro is a bit different. The budget-friendly smartwatch with a 1.43-inch OLED display is even cheaper than usual at Amazon, where it costs $69 in every color (the price fluctuates between $79 and $99). Some notable […]

item.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
item.description: OpenAI's first hardware device is reported to be a screenless, AI-guided smart speaker that can move. Weird enough for you?

item.title: The UK Is Planning a Social Media Curfew for 16- and 17-Year-Olds
item.description: The restrictions, which can be turned off, will include a crackdown on “addictive” app features and will be in addition to a total ban on children under 16 accessing platforms like TikTok and YouTube.

item.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
item.description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

item.title: Quote of the day by Linux creator Linus Torvalds: 'Nvidia has been the single worst company we've ever dealt with' — airing frustrations at walled gardens
item.description: The Linux creator had a particularly tough time with Nvidia throughout history – but things have changed dramatically since then

item.title: Don't forget, you can save 50% on Criterion 4K Blu-rays at Amazon and Barnes & Noble right now — the sale is for a limited time, so don't miss it if you're looking to add to your collection
item.description: Half price on all Criterion discs? Oh god, where do I even start?

item.title: See your kitchen or bathroom renovation in 3D with this $20 software
item.description: Get a lifetime license to ArchiMaster 3D Kitchen & Bath for $19.99 (reg. $59.99) and visualize renovation ideas before construction begins.

item.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
item.description: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

item.title: The WGA is also suing to block Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
item.description: It seems some people take issue with one billionaire family overseeing a third of the US' entertainment media.

item.title: Réseaux sociaux | Le gouvernement britannique annonce un couvre-feu numérique pour les adolescents
item.description: Le gouvernement britannique a annoncé mardi un couvre-feu nocturne entre minuit et 6 h du matin sur les réseaux sociaux pour les adolescents de 16 et 17 ans, une mesure qui complète l’interdiction des réseaux aux moins de 16 ans annoncée en juin.

item.title: New TV guidelines could change how women athletes are shown during live sports
item.description: New broadcasting guidelines encourage camera operators to avoid angles that sexualize women athletes and instead keep the focus on athletic performance. From Michael Phelps breaking records to Alysa Liu’s emotive gold medal ice skating performance, broadcast television has allowed athletes to bring their remarkable talents to a global audience. But for some, particularly women athletes, broadcast may also become a stage for compromising camera angles—and some are looking to change that....

item.title: Windows 11’s big patch Tuesday allows you to hold off on updates for longer
item.description: Microsoft just released a long list of improvements for Windows 11 as part of its bigger patch Tuesdays, and that includes the ability to pause updates indefinitely, as reported earlier by Windows Central. This option rolled out to Windows Insiders earlier this year, allowing you to hold off on updates for up to 35 days […]

item.title: Lawsuit claims Meta used AI to unfairly target employees for layoffs
item.description: A group of 26 recently laid off Meta workers accuse the giant of using AI software to target specific categories of employees.

item.title: OpenAI may announce a ChatGPT smart speaker this year
item.description: OpenAI's first device is set to be a smart speaker that lets you talk with ChatGPT, according to a report from Bloomberg. The device apparently won't have a screen, but will use a camera and additional sensors to "understand" your environment. The report comes just days after Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI that accused […]

item.title: OnePlus smartphones could leave U.S. and Europe this week
item.description: One of the best options for affordable flagship phones could stop selling its wares in the west as soon as this week.

item.title: Former employees sue over Meta's alleged use of biased AI systems during layoffs
item.description: Meta cut its workforce by 10 percent in May.

item.title: Germany confirms drone-destroying laser weapon for German Navy by 2029 — 100Kw version will zap 1000+ mph supersonic missiles for $1 a shot
item.description: Germany plans operational naval laser weapons by 2029 after extensive trials, while engineers continue addressing technical and production challenges ahead.

item.title: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan has slashed 40% of jobs in some departments, thanks to AI
item.description: Still, Dimon seems to believe AI won’t radically reshape the bank’s workforce. JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has repeatedly suggested that AI adoption would not drive widespread layoffs at the bank. But on Tuesday, he disclosed that JPMorgan had made significant cuts to a number of departments due to AI.
